sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: Getafe have had a mixed run in their last 5 matches, with 1 win, 1 draw, and 3 losses, showing inconsistency in attack (scoring just 3 goals) and often conceding first. Espanyol, on the other hand, are in better shape with 3 wins and 2 losses in their last 5, displaying a knack for late goals and defensive resilience, conceding fewer goals overall.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: In the last two encounters between these teams, each side has claimed a 1-0 victory, with matches typically being low-scoring and tight. Espanyol hold a slight edge in historical matchups with 3 wins to Getafe's 2 in the last 5 head-to-heads, suggesting a closely contested battle.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Espanyol sit higher in the standings (5th with 27 points) compared to Getafe (8th with 20 points), reflecting better consistency and attacking output (19 goals vs. Getafe's 13). Getafe's strength lies in their defensive structure (7th fewest goals conceded), while Espanyol's ability to score late could exploit Getafe's tendency to concede in the final stages.

sections.subtitle: Key Players and Tactics
sections.text: For Getafe, Borja Mayoral (4 goals) will be crucial in breaking through Espanyol's defense, while their high tackle count (2nd in the league) suggests a physical approach. Espanyol's Pere Milla (5 goals) and Edu Expósito (4 assists) are key threats, with their team likely to capitalize on set pieces and counter-attacks.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Espanyol's superior form, league position, and ability to grind out results, they edge out as favorites per Sofascore's 42% away win probability. Getafe's home advantage and defensive solidity might keep it close, but Espanyol's recent momentum could see them nick a narrow victory.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between Getafe and Espanyol at the Coliseum Alfonso Pérez ended in a goalless draw. Neither side managed to break the deadlock despite some promising moments. Espanyol dominated possession and showed more intent in the final third, creating a notable chance but failing to convert. Getafe, on the other hand, struggled to create clear-cut opportunities but remained solid defensively despite disciplinary issues with multiple bookings.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Getafe
halftimeSections.text: Getafe had a challenging first half in terms of attacking output, failing to register a shot on target. Their entries into the final third were decent, but they lacked precision in the penalty area to create dangerous moments. Defensively, they held their own with a strong showing in aerial duels and a reasonable share of ground duels won. However, their disciplinary record was a concern, picking up several yellow cards which could impact their approach in the second half. They will need to sharpen their attack while maintaining defensive discipline to turn the tide.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Team performance: Espanyol
halftimeSections.text: Espanyol looked more composed in the first half, controlling the ball for longer periods and making more frequent entries into the final third. They created a big chance but couldn’t capitalize, which might frustrate them going into the break. Their duel success rate was slightly below par, particularly in the air, which could be an area to exploit for Getafe. With a higher expected goals tally, Espanyol showed more attacking threat and will likely push to convert their dominance into goals in the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum between the two sides. Espanyol started brightly with early attacking spells, showing intent to control the game from the outset. Getafe responded with a few bursts of pressure, particularly in the middle stages of the half, where they managed to push forward consistently for a brief period. However, Espanyol regained the upper hand with sustained attacking momentum as the half progressed, dominating the latter minutes with several dangerous forays into Getafe’s territory. These shifts suggest Espanyol might carry more confidence into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key players to watch
halftimeSections.text: For Getafe, Adrian Liso stood out with his contributions in key passes and duels won, potentially becoming a creative spark if they can build on his involvement. On the Espanyol side, Pere Milla was pivotal in creating chances, and his ability to set up plays could be decisive if they capitalize on opportunities. Fernando Calero also showed threat from set pieces but missed a big chance, which might motivate him to make amends in the second half.
